evidence.scientific_clarity: Ridgeline KDEs for all 6 clusters are stacked with mild overlap (0.55), y-axis labels each ridge, x-axis labeled — brief's claim of progressive expression shift is directly readable.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type3 (bitmap glyphs confirmed), SVG uses path outlines not <text> elements — text is non-editable in both vector outputs.
evidence.palette: ColorBrewer Blues sequential (#c6dbef→#084594) — colorblind-safe, no red/green directional confusion, appropriate for ordered sequential clusters.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,5) with tight_layout and overlap=0.55 is reasonable; top/right/left spines removed cleanly, but no x-axis tick grid or minor visual polish to fill the plot area.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption states the progressive shift finding and matches the figure; no legend needed (y-axis labels clusters); bandwidth parameter documented in caption — minor gap: no numerical quantification of the shift.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed Type3 (bitmap) fonts only — pdf.fonttype=42 not set, so text is not selectable/editable in the PDF; SVG likewise uses glyph path outlines instead of <text> nodes.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (47 KB), figure.png (50 KB, 1035×732 px), figure.svg (186 KB), caption.tex (343 B), gen_figure.py (1955 B) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies plot type, kernel, bandwidth, normalization, and the directional finding (progressive shift to higher expression); lacks a concrete numerical claim (e.g., median shift magnitude between Cluster 1 and 6).

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe ColorBrewer Blues sequential palette is well-matched to the ordered cluster structure.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; caption correctly describes the figure and includes methodological detail (bandwidth, normalization).
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are both missing — fonts are bitmapped Type3 glyphs in the PDF and path outlines in the SVG, making text non-selectable and non-editable in all vector outputs.

Caption lacks a specific numerical finding (e.g., 'Cluster 6 peaks ~X log-units higher than Cluster 1'), and the default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout with no publication-quality font substitution.
